full_text:
Installation

macOS

Install Docker Desktop for Mac:
 https://docs.docker.com/desktop/install/mac-install/

🧭 Building and Pushing a Data-Layer Docker Image (Complete Guide)

📂 Folder:

/Users/sarpalec/Documents/Docker/PRISM_ref/

Your reference data files should be here.

Step 1 – Prepare folder structure

Open Terminal and navigate to your project directory:
cd /Users/sarpalec/Documents/Docker/PRISM_ref/

Create a data/ folder (required by the Dockerfile):
mkdir -p data

Move all reference files inside the new folder:
mv * data/ 2>/dev/null || true

(This ignores errors if some files are already in data/.)

Confirm the structure:
ls

You should see:
data/

Inside:
ls data

You should see your reference files.

Step 2 – Create the Dockerfile

Create a file named Dockerfile.data in the same directory.

You can do this easily with one command:

cat > Dockerfile.data << 'EOF'

FROM ubuntu:22.04

# Add reference dataset

COPY data/ /opt/reference/

RUN chmod -R a+rX /opt/reference

# Helpful env var

ENV REF_DIR=/opt/reference

# Default to a shell (you can override with a command)

CMD ["/bin/sh"]

EOF

Check that it worked:

cat Dockerfile.data

You should see:

FROM ubuntu:22.04

COPY data/ /opt/reference/

RUN chmod -R a+rX /opt/reference

ENV REF_DIR=/opt/reference

CMD ["/bin/sh"]

Step 3 – Build the Docker image

Make sure you’re still in the same folder as the Dockerfile and the data/ directory.

Run:

docker build -f Dockerfile.data -t prism-ref-data .

✅ This creates an image called prism-ref-data.

Step 4 – Verify the image

Check that your data is inside /opt/reference in the container:

docker run --rm -ti prism-ref-data ls -lh /opt/reference

You should see your data files listed.
Optionally, explore interactively:

docker run --rm -it prism-ref-data /bin/bash

docker run --rm -it prism-data /bin/bash

When done, type:

exit

Step 5 – Login to Azure and ACR

Login to your Azure account:
az login

EXT19675019@hustietoallas.fi

Login to the iCAN public Azure Container Registry (ACR):
az acr login -n hfdtu1822021icanpubliccr

Step 6 – Tag and push the image

Tag your local image for Azure:

docker tag prism-ref-data

hfdtu1822021icanpubliccr.azurecr.io/prism-ref-data

Push it to the cloud:

docker push hfdtu1822021icanpubliccr.azurecr.io/prism-ref-data

docker push hfdtu1822021icanpubliccr.azurecr.io/prism-data

This uploads the image (and your /opt/reference data) to the iCAN registry.

Step 7 – Confirm the push

List your local Docker images:

docker images

You should see:

hfdtu1822021icanpubliccr.azurecr.io/prism-ref-data latest <IMAGE_ID> <SIZE>

prism-ref-data latest <IMAGE_ID> <SIZE>

You can also pull the image later from anywhere:

docker pull hfdtu1822021icanpubliccr.azurecr.io/prism-ref-data

✅ Quick Summary

💡 Notes

You must always run docker build from the folder containing both Dockerfile.data and the data/ directory.

FROM ubuntu:22.04 is a generic base; if your reference data is meant for an R or Python image, replace it later with the appropriate base (e.g., FROM rocker/r-ver:4.3.2).

After pushing, you can safely delete the local image if needed with:
docker rmi prism-ref-data

✅ Fix: Rebuild the image for amd64 and re-push it

You can easily do this from your Mac using Docker’s multi-architecture build support.

Step 1. Go to your project folder again

cd /Users/sarpalec/Documents/Docker/PRISM_ref/

Step 2. Rebuild with the correct architecture

Run this command (adds the platform flag):

docker buildx build --platform linux/amd64 -f Dockerfile.data -t prism-ref-data .

If you get an error like “buildx not found,” enable BuildKit with:

export DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1

and retry.

Step 3. Verify the image platform

Check with:

docker inspect prism-ref-data | grep -A 5 Architecture

You should see:

"Architecture": "amd64"

Step 4. Tag and push again to ACR

az login

az acr login -n hfdtu1822021icanpubliccr

docker tag prism-ref-data hfdtu1822021icanpubliccr.azurecr.io/prism-ref-data

docker push hfdtu1822021icanpubliccr.azurecr.io/prism-ref-data

Step 5. Wait for sync to the private registry

Give it 10–15 min for the public → private ACR sync.

Step 6. On iCAN, pull again

docker pull hfdtu1822021icanprivatecr.azurecr.io/prism-ref-data:latest

This time it should pull successfully (because it now includes an amd64 manifest).

Step 7. Run as before

docker run -u "$(id -u):$(id -g)" -v $HOME/mounts/research:/research -ti hfdtu1822021icanprivatecr.azurecr.io/prism-ref-data:latest /bin/sh

Then inside the container:

ls /opt/reference

4) On iCAN Linux Desktop: login + pull from private ACR

Set artifact:

export ARTIFACT=prism-data

Login:

az login

newgrp docker

az acr login -n hfdtu1822021icanprivatecr

newgrp "domain users"

Check tags exist in private:

az acr repository show-tags -n hfdtu1822021icanprivatecr --repository prism-data -o table

Pull:

docker pull hfdtu1822021icanprivatecr.azurecr.io/$ARTIFACT:latest

# or

docker pull hfdtu1822021icanprivatecr.azurecr.io/$ARTIFACT:amd64

5) If pull/run by tag fails: pull/run by digest (worked)

If I get:

manifest not found, or

any weird tag behavior

Get the digest for latest:

az acr repository show-manifests \

-n hfdtu1822021icanprivatecr \

--repository prism-data \

-o table

Copy the digest sha256:....

Then run using digest:

docker run --rm -ti \

hfdtu1822021icanprivatecr.azurecr.io/prism-data@sha256:<PASTE_DIGEST> \

/bin/sh -lc 'uname -m; ls -lah /opt/reference | head'

Expected:

uname -m prints x86_64

/opt/reference contains my files (e.g., sc_data.rds, sc_matrix.rds, etc.)

6) Copy reference folder into Research drive (permanent storage)

My target folder on iCAN:

~/mounts/research/users/SPE

Copy from image → research drive:

docker run --rm \

-u "$(id -u):$(id -g)" \

-v $HOME/mounts/research:/research \

hfdtu1822021icanprivatecr.azurecr.io/prism-data@sha256:<PASTE_DIGEST> \

cp -r /opt/reference /research/users/SPE/

Verify outside container:

ls -lah ~/mounts/research/users/SPE/reference | head

du -sh ~/mounts/research/users/SPE/reference

7) Quick troubleshooting

Docker daemon not running (on laptop)

Error: Cannot connect to the Docker daemon...
Fix: Start Docker Desktop.

“no build stage in current context”

Dockerfile missing FROM ... at top.

“zsh: no such file or directory: name”

Don’t use <name> brackets. Use a real name: -t prism-data

“no matching manifest for linux/amd64”

Built for arm64; rebuild/push with buildx --platform linux/amd64.

Tag exists but pull fails

Use digest method from step 5.

8) Canonical image names

Public push target:

hfdtu1822021icanpubliccr.azurecr.io/prism-data:latest

Private pull target:

hfdtu1822021icanprivatecr.azurecr.io/prism-data:latest

or digest: .../prism-data@sha256:...

Data inside image:

/opt/reference

$REF_DIR (if ENV was set)

Step | Command | Purpose
1 | cd /Users/sarpalec/Documents/Docker/PRISM_ref/ | Go to working directory
2 | mkdir -p data & move files | Create and organize data folder
3 | cat > Dockerfile.data << 'EOF' ... EOF | Create Dockerfile
4 | docker build -f Dockerfile.data -t prism-ref-data . | Build local image
5 | docker run --rm -ti prism-ref-data ls /opt/reference | Verify contents
6 | az login & az acr login -n hfdtu1822021icanpubliccr | Login to Azure ACR
7 | docker tag prism-ref-data hfdtu1822021icanpubliccr.azurecr.io/prism-ref-data | Tag for registry
8 | docker push hfdtu1822021icanpubliccr.azurecr.io/prism-ref-data | Push to ACR
